paragraphs.en: At some point, Layla had stopped living her life and started performing it. She couldn't pinpoint exactly when the shift had happened — somewhere between her twenties and her early thirties, the boundary between experience and content had quietly dissolved. She still went to restaurants, saw exhibitions, visited friends. But she caught herself, increasingly, thinking: will this photograph well?
paragraphs.fr: À un moment, Layla avait cessé de vivre sa vie et avait commencé à la mettre en scène. Elle ne pouvait pas identifier exactement quand ce basculement s'était produit — quelque part entre la vingtaine et le début de la trentaine, la frontière entre l'expérience et le contenu s'était doucement dissoute. Elle allait encore au restaurant, voyait des expositions, rendait visite à des amis. Mais elle se surprenait, de plus en plus souvent, à penser : est-ce que ça fera une belle photo ?

paragraphs.en: Her account had thirty thousand followers. This was, she knew, simultaneously a lot and not very much. Enough to attract occasional free products from brands she had never heard of; not enough to turn the whole thing into a living. She had a day job in marketing, which meant she spent eight hours helping other people curate their image, then came home and spent two more hours curating her own.
paragraphs.fr: Son compte comptait trente mille abonnés. C'était, elle le savait, à la fois beaucoup et pas grand-chose. Assez pour attirer des produits gratuits occasionnels de marques dont elle n'avait jamais entendu parler ; pas assez pour en faire un vrai travail. Elle avait un emploi à temps plein dans le marketing, ce qui signifiait qu'elle passait huit heures à aider d'autres personnes à soigner leur image, puis rentrait chez elle et passait deux heures de plus à soigner la sienne.

paragraphs.en: The irony was not lost on her. She had studied it, written about it, given a presentation on it at university: the way social media created a gap between the self you were and the self you projected. She had used words like 'performance' and 'fragmentation'. And yet here she was, spending forty minutes finding the right angle for a photograph of a plate of food.
paragraphs.fr: L'ironie ne lui avait pas échappé. Elle l'avait étudié, écrit dessus, présenté à l'université : la façon dont les réseaux sociaux créaient un écart entre le soi que l'on était et le soi que l'on projetait. Elle avait utilisé des mots comme « mise en scène » et « fragmentation ». Et pourtant la voilà, passant quarante minutes à trouver le bon angle pour photographier une assiette de nourriture.

paragraphs.en: The turning point, if there was one, came on a Tuesday in February. She had spent the morning photographing her breakfast — overnight oats with a swirl of almond butter and three precisely arranged blueberries — and posting it with a caption about 'nourishing the body and soul'. As she hit publish, she became aware, with sudden clarity, that she had not actually eaten the oats. They had gone cold. She ate them standing at the kitchen counter, looking at her phone, reading the comments that said things like 'so beautiful' and 'goals'.
paragraphs.fr: Le tournant, s'il y en eut un, arriva un mardi de février. Elle avait passé la matinée à photographier son petit-déjeuner — des flocons d'avoine préparés la veille avec un tourbillon de beurre d'amande et trois myrtilles soigneusement disposées — et à le poster avec une légende sur « nourrir le corps et l'âme ». En appuyant sur publier, elle prit conscience, avec une clarté soudaine, qu'elle n'avait pas réellement mangé les flocons d'avoine. Ils avaient refroidi. Elle les mangea debout au comptoir de la cuisine, regardant son téléphone, lisant les commentaires qui disaient des choses comme « tellement beau » et « goals ».

paragraphs.en: She didn't delete her account. She wasn't ready for that, or perhaps wasn't brave enough. But she started leaving her phone in her bag at restaurants. She went to an exhibition and didn't take a single photograph. She had a long walk in the rain and decided not to describe it to anyone. These small acts of privacy felt — she searched for the right word — nourishing. The real kind.
paragraphs.fr: Elle ne supprima pas son compte. Elle n'était pas prête pour ça, ou peut-être pas assez courageuse. Mais elle commença à laisser son téléphone dans son sac au restaurant. Elle alla à une exposition et ne prit pas une seule photo. Elle fit une longue promenade sous la pluie et décida de ne la décrire à personne. Ces petits actes de vie privée lui semblaient — elle cherchait le bon mot — nourrissants. Le vrai genre.

paragraphs.en: The strange thing was that nobody noticed. Her posting frequency dropped by half. The numbers ticked up anyway. Apparently, less was more.
paragraphs.fr: La chose étrange était que personne ne remarqua rien. Sa fréquence de publication chuta de moitié. Les chiffres continuèrent malgré tout d'augmenter. Apparemment, moins c'était plus.

vocabulary.definition_en: identifier ou définir avec une grande précision
vocabulary.definition_fr: identifier précisément, mettre le doigt sur
vocabulary.example: She couldn't pinpoint exactly when her attitude towards work had changed.

vocabulary.definition_en: disparaître progressivement ; faire disparaître quelque chose
vocabulary.example: The distinction between work and leisure has slowly dissolved since we started working from home.

vocabulary.definition_en: sélectionner et présenter soigneusement du contenu ou des objets, à l'origine dans un contexte muséal, maintenant largement utilisé pour l'image personnelle
vocabulary.definition_fr: sélectionner soigneusement, construire (une image), mettre en valeur
vocabulary.example: She spent hours curating her Instagram feed to create a specific aesthetic.

vocabulary.definition_en: une situation où le contraire de ce qui est attendu ou voulu se produit, souvent avec un effet légèrement comique ou amer
vocabulary.example: The irony was that the happiest people she knew were the least active on social media.

vocabulary.definition_en: présenter ou afficher une image ou impression particulière de soi-même aux autres
vocabulary.example: She worked hard to project an image of confidence and competence.

vocabulary.definition_en: la qualité d'être clair, transparent ou facile à comprendre ; un moment soudain de compréhension
vocabulary.example: She experienced a moment of sudden clarity and knew exactly what she had to do.

vocabulary.definition_en: fournir la nourriture et les substances nécessaires à la croissance et à la santé ; (au figuré) soutenir et enrichir
vocabulary.definition_fr: nourrir (au sens propre et figuré), enrichir, alimenter
vocabulary.example: A good conversation can nourish the soul as much as a good meal.

vocabulary.definition_en: le rythme auquel quelque chose se produit ; à quelle fréquence quelque chose arrive
vocabulary.example: She reduced the frequency of her posts to once a week.

vocabulary.definition_en: le processus de division de quelque chose en petites parties déconnectées
vocabulary.example: The constant switching between apps leads to a fragmentation of attention.

vocabulary.definition_en: l'idée que la simplicité et la retenue produisent de meilleurs résultats que l'excès
vocabulary.definition_fr: moins c'est plus, le minimalisme est plus efficace
vocabulary.example: With public speaking, less is often more — a few strong points beat a long list.

grammar_points.title: Catching yourself doing something: 'she caught herself thinking'
grammar_points.explanation: **'Catch yourself + gérondif'** décrit le moment où vous vous surprenez à faire quelque chose involontairement ou presque inconsciemment — souvent avec une pointe de surprise. Cela implique un écart entre le soi conscient et le soi automatique.
examples: 'She **caught herself** thinking: will this photograph well?' → involuntary thought
'I **caught myself** checking my phone before I'd even got out of bed.'
'He **caught himself** smiling despite the difficult situation.'
Compare: 'She noticed she was thinking...' (neutral) vs. 'She caught herself thinking...' (surprise, slight self-awareness)

grammar_points.title: Passive voice for impersonal distance: 'it was not lost on her'
grammar_points.explanation: **'Not be lost on someone'** est une structure passive idiomatique signifiant 'quelqu'un a parfaitement compris ou perçu quelque chose'. Dans la prose formelle, l'utilisation du passif crée un ton élégant et légèrement détaché — comme si l'ironie existait indépendamment et que Layla l'avait simplement 'reçue'.
examples: 'The irony was **not lost on her**.' → She was fully aware of the irony.
'The significance of the moment was **not lost on** the audience.' → They all understood.
'His frustration was **not lost on** his colleagues.' → They could all sense it.
This is a fixed expression — always use 'not lost on' + person.

grammar_points.title: Reported thought with 'apparently': expressing surprised realisation
grammar_points.explanation: **'Apparently'** peut être utilisé en début de phrase pour rapporter une découverte ou tirer une conclusion quelque peu ironique. Il est également utilisé dans le **discours indirect libre** — une technique littéraire où le narrateur adopte la perspective du personnage sans utiliser de guillemets.
examples: '**Apparently**, less was more.' → Layla's ironic realisation, presented as her thought
'**Apparently**, no one had noticed she was struggling.' → narrator adopts character's surprise
This technique avoids 'she thought that...' and makes the prose feel more immediate.
Tip: 'Apparently' in spoken English often signals scepticism: 'Apparently he's an expert.' = I'm not sure I believe it.

expressions.expression: the irony was not lost on her
expressions.meaning_fr: elle ne manquait pas de percevoir l'ironie, l'ironie n'avait pas échappé
expressions.example: The irony was not lost on her — she had spent years studying this exact phenomenon.
expressions.note: Expression figée. Signifie que la personne était pleinement consciente du contraste ironique de sa situation.

expressions.expression: to turn something into a living
expressions.meaning_fr: en faire son gagne-pain, en vivre
expressions.example: She had thirty thousand followers — not enough to turn the whole thing into a living.
expressions.note: Signifie gagner assez d'argent avec quelque chose pour subvenir à ses besoins. 'She turned her hobby into a living' = elle en a fait son métier.

expressions.example: The turning point came on a Tuesday in February.
expressions.note: Un moment clé où les choses commencent à changer. Très courant dans la narration et les essais narratifs.

expressions.example: As she hit publish, she suddenly felt doubt.
expressions.note: Expression moderne du monde numérique — 'hit' signifie ici 'appuyer sur' un bouton. Très courant dans l'anglais de tous les jours.

expressions.meaning_fr: moins c'est plus, le minimalisme est souvent plus efficace
expressions.example: Apparently, less was more — fewer posts led to more followers.
expressions.note: Souvent attribué à l'architecte Mies van der Rohe. Largement utilisé dans les contextes du design, de la communication et du mode de vie.

written.question: What is the key shift that Layla experiences at the start of the story?
options: She loses her job in marketing and must rely on social media income.
She moves from experiencing her life to performing it for an audience.
She gains thirty thousand followers overnight and feels overwhelmed.
She decides to study social media at university.
written.explanation: The opening line states: 'At some point, Layla had stopped living her life and started performing it.' This is the central shift the story explores.

written.question: What is the turning point in the story?
options: Layla decides to delete her account permanently.
Layla realises she photographed her breakfast but never actually ate it.
Layla gives a presentation about social media at university.
written.explanation: The moment of clarity comes when she realises the oats had gone cold — she had photographed them but never eaten them.

written.sentence: She ________ smiling when she read his message, even though she had been upset all morning.
written.hint: An expression meaning 'to notice yourself doing something involuntarily'.

written.sentence: The significance of his words was not ________ her — she understood exactly what he meant.
written.hint: A fixed expression meaning 'fully understood or appreciated by'.

written.sentence: She couldn't ________ exactly when she had started to feel unhappy.
written.hint: A verb meaning 'to identify with precision'.

written.french: La frontière entre travail et vie personnelle s'était doucement dissoute pendant la pandémie.
written.suggested_answer: The boundary between work and personal life had quietly dissolved during the pandemic.

written.french: Elle passait des heures à soigner son image en ligne, ce dont elle percevait tout à fait l'ironie.
written.suggested_answer: She spent hours curating her image online, and the irony was not lost on her.
key_vocabulary: the irony was not lost on her

written.french: Elle se surprit à se demander si cette expérience ferait une bonne photo.
written.suggested_answer: She caught herself wondering whether this experience would make a good photograph.

oral.instruction: Read the following passage, which describes Layla's moment of realisation. The tone should be quietly observational — detached but with an underlying sadness.
oral.text: As she hit publish, she became aware, with sudden clarity, that she had not actually eaten the oats. They had gone cold. She ate them standing at the kitchen counter, looking at her phone, reading the comments that said things like 'so beautiful' and 'goals'.
oral.focus: Pause briefly after 'clarity' and after 'cold'. The list at the end — 'standing at the kitchen counter, looking at her phone, reading the comments' — should feel accumulative and slightly mechanical.
phonetic_tips: goals: stress on the single syllable for ironic effect

oral.question: Do you think social media encourages people to perform their lives rather than live them? Give your view.
oral.suggested_answer: I think there's real truth to that idea, though the extent varies a lot between people and platforms. The problem isn't the technology itself, but the feedback loop it creates — likes and comments reward certain behaviours, and over time those behaviours reshape how we experience things in the first place. I've caught myself thinking 'this would make a good post' before I've even fully absorbed the moment I'm in. That said, I don't think it's entirely negative. Social media can also be a way of sharing joy, connecting with people, or documenting a life in meaningful ways. The issue is when the documentation becomes the primary purpose.

oral.question: Describe a time when you did something just for yourself, without sharing it or telling anyone. How did it feel?
oral.suggested_answer: Last summer I went to a concert alone — it was someone I'd wanted to see for years. I almost posted about it before I went, but then I decided not to. And actually, not sharing it changed the experience entirely. I paid more attention. I wasn't thinking about how to describe it or what photo to take. When it was over, I didn't want to talk about it immediately — I wanted to keep it to myself for a while. I realise that sounds odd, but it felt like a small act of privacy, and it was surprisingly nourishing.
